fact: August 20 is exactly 232 days into the year, which means there are only 133 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for cicadas to be screaming their loudest - some can buzz as loud as a motorcycle!
The sun rises about 2 minutes later than it did just a week ago, and summer is secretly sneaking away minute by minute.
August 20 falls right in the middle of Leo season, when the zodiac lion is roaring at full strength until August 22.
Sunflowers across the Northern Hemisphere are at their absolute peak right now, with some giant varieties towering over 12 feet tall!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere countries, August 20 is deep winter, and some places might even have snow on the ground.
This date sits exactly 61 days after the summer solstice in the north, making it the perfect mirror to late June.
Monarch butterflies in North America are starting their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico around this time - talk about a road trip!
The moon phase on August 20 changes every year, but when it's a full moon on this date, it's called the Sturgeon Moon because fish are fattest right now.
In many northern regions, this is when maple trees start their color-changing magic, even though most people won't notice for weeks.
August 20 marks the time when many birds begin their pre-migration fattening frenzy, stuffing themselves like tiny feathered sumo wrestlers.
